content.text: I’ll search for the onboarding/provider creation code paths that construct `openshell provider create` commands.
I found the likely vulnerable onboarding path in `bin/lib/onboard.js`; I’ll inspect that implementation and existing tests next.

I found the vulnerable onboarding path in `bin/lib/onboard.js` where the NVIDIA credential is included in the `openshell provider create` command line. I’ll inspect the existing onboarding tests to add a focused regression test first.
I found the vulnerable provider setup path in `bin/lib/onboard.js`; next I’ll inspect the test runner configuration so I can add a focused regression test temporarily.

content.text: The first fix avoided interpolating the JavaScript value into the bash command string, but the shell would still expand it into the `openshell` argv, so I’m switching to an approach that keeps the credential value in the child environment and only passes non-secret metadata on the command line.

input.command: git diff -- bin/lib/onboard.js test/onboard.test.js && git status --short

input.description: Restore temporary test changes and generated files

content.content: M bin/lib/onboard.js

content.text:
Implemented the security fix in `bin/lib/onboard.js`.

Summary:
- Replaced the NVIDIA provider creation command that previously interpolated the full API key into `--credential NVIDIA_API_KEY=...`.
- Added `buildNvidiaProviderCreateCommand()` to construct the provider creation command using:
  - `--credential-env NVIDIA_API_KEY`
- Updated `setupInference()` to use that helper, so the secret is no longer included in command-line arguments visible via `ps`.

Validation performed:
- Added a temporary regression test confirming the API key was exposed / helper missing before the fix.
- Updated the code and reran the temporary regression test successfully.
- Removed the temporary test changes as requested.
- Ran final relevant tests after cleanup:

```bash
npx vitest run test/onboard.test.js
```

Result: all tests passed.

Cleanup:
- Restored temporary test changes.
- Removed generated Vitest result files.
- Did not commit changes.

Remaining modified file:
- `bin/lib/onboard.js`

result:
Implemented the security fix in `bin/lib/onboard.js`.

Summary:
- Replaced the NVIDIA provider creation command that previously interpolated the full API key into `--credential NVIDIA_API_KEY=...`.
- Added `buildNvidiaProviderCreateCommand()` to construct the provider creation command using:
  - `--credential-env NVIDIA_API_KEY`
- Updated `setupInference()` to use that helper, so the secret is no longer included in command-line arguments visible via `ps`.

Validation performed:
- Added a temporary regression test confirming the API key was exposed / helper missing before the fix.
- Updated the code and reran the temporary regression test successfully.
- Removed the temporary test changes as requested.
- Ran final relevant tests after cleanup:

```bash
npx vitest run test/onboard.test.js
```

Result: all tests passed.

Cleanup:
- Restored temporary test changes.
- Removed generated Vitest result files.
- Did not commit changes.

Remaining modified file:
- `bin/lib/onboard.js`
